hallucination.rationale: The note states omeprazole '20 milligrams twice a day' when the transcript clearly says 'once daily.' This is a fabricated detail that changes the clinical plan. The note also lacks structure and reads as informal thinking rather than a clinical note.

instruction_following.rationale: The output is not a clinical note at all — it is a series of informal 'think' blocks with no structured sections, no proper formatting, and no adherence to the template instructions. It completely ignores the template structure and instructions.

completeness.rationale: While some key elements are loosely mentioned (burning chest sensation, GERD, omeprazole, lifestyle modifications, follow-up), many specific details are missing: 3-week duration, radiation to throat, specific trigger foods (tomato-based sauces, pasta, coffee 2-3 cups/day), negative dysphagia/odynophagia/hematemesis/melena/weight loss, timing instruction (30 min before breakfast), 2-week full effect timeline, not eating 2-3 hours before lying down.
missed_items: radiation to throat with sour taste
take on empty stomach 30 minutes before breakfast
not eating within 2-3 hours of lying down

template_adherence.rationale: The output has no structured sections whatsoever. It does not follow the template at all — no Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan sections, no headings, no proper clinical note format.

entity_marking.rationale: No entity marking was attempted anywhere in the output. No {{drug:...}} or {{condition:...}} syntax was used.

duplication.rationale: The output contains three separate <think> blocks that largely repeat the same content, constituting significant duplication.
